title: ArchLinterNet dependency architecture policy
description: Schema for one explicitly selected ArchLinterNet root policy, regardless of filename.

version.description: Policy schema version. Current supported version is 1.

layers.description: Named layer definitions referenced by contracts.

external_dependencies.description: Named vendor/framework dependency groups referenced by external dependency contracts.

packages.description: Named NuGet package groups referenced by package dependency and package allow-only contracts.

legacy_runtime_layers.description: Namespace prefixes used by dependency contracts with forbidden_legacy_runtime enabled.

imports.description: Ordered explicit local policy fragments, resolved relative to this document.

namespace.description: Namespace prefix or constrained glob pattern. Supports '*' as a single-segment wildcard (e.g. 'App.Features.*'). Literal prefixes use exact or child-namespace semantics. See documentation for full glob syntax.

namespace_suffix.description: Optional namespace suffix constraint, matched after the namespace prefix.

external.description: When true, suppresses the empty-layer configuration diagnostic for this layer. Use for namespaces whose assemblies may not be available in the scan environment (external SDKs, engine types, platform-conditional namespaces).

selector.description: Matches types by discovered role and optional exact metadata in addition to an optional namespace constraint. When both are present, both constraints must match.

role.description: Exact-match role name assigned by classification. Role names are opaque strings; no fixed role catalog is enforced by this schema.

metadata.description: Optional exact-match, AND-combined metadata key/value constraints. All declared keys must match.

role.description: Exact-match role name assigned by classification, matched directly against a candidate type's resolved role rather than through a declared layers.<name>.

metadata.description: Optional metadata constraints, each evaluated by exactly one of four deterministic operators (checked in this order): a YAML sequence of literal scalars ('in' — matches any listed entry), the literal string '*' ('any' — matches any resolved value provided the key is present), a string matching '!{source.metadata.<key>}' ('not-equal-to-source' — matches when the candidate's value differs from the current source type's own resolved value for <key>; only meaningful on forbidden/allowed/exclude selectors), or any other scalar ('exact' literal match).

anyOf.description: not-equal-to-source reference to the current source type's own resolved metadata value for <key>.
in operator: matches if the candidate's resolved value equals any listed entry.

role.description: Exact-match role name assigned by classification, matched directly against a candidate type's resolved role rather than through a declared layers.<name>.

metadata.description: Optional metadata constraints for the contract's own `source` selector. Supports the 'exact', 'any' ('*'), and 'in' operators — not-equal-to-source ('!{source.metadata.<key>}') is rejected here because the source selector has no other source to compare against; it is valid only on forbidden/allowed/exclude selectors (see contextSelector).

anyOf.description: in operator: matches if the candidate's resolved value equals any listed entry.

namespace_prefixes.description: Namespace prefixes matched with exact or child-namespace semantics against referenced external types.

type_prefixes.description: Full type-name prefixes matched against referenced external types.

package_ids.description: NuGet package IDs matched exactly (case-insensitively) against declared PackageReference items.

package_prefixes.description: NuGet package ID prefixes matched with exact or dot-segment child semantics (case-insensitively) against declared PackageReference items.

classification.description: Semantic-classification-model design (see openspec/changes/archive/2026-07-10-design-semantic-classification-model). `attributes`/`assembly_attributes`/`inheritance`/`namespace` extraction is implemented (see openspec/specs/attribute-role-extraction and openspec/specs/semantic-classification-model). `precedence` is enforced at runtime for these four implemented sources (a source it omits is disabled; its `yaml_override`/`path` entries have no effect either way, since those two sources are not yet implemented). `path`, `overrides`, and `exclusions` remain schema-acceptance only; declaring `overrides`/`exclusions` has no runtime effect, and declaring `path` produces a deferred-support diagnostic but no role assignment yet.

precedence.description: Subsequence of the fixed source order [yaml_override, type_attribute, assembly_attribute, inheritance, namespace, path], preserving relative order with no duplicates. Sources omitted are disabled. Defaults to all six sources in the fixed order. Enumerated as an explicit oneOf of the 63 valid ordered, duplicate-free subsequences so reordering or repeating a source is rejected by the schema.

attribute.description: Full type name of the attribute, matched against attributes declared or referenced in scanned code. No binary ArchLinterNet annotation dependency is required.

metadata.description: Values use the metadata extraction syntax: constructor[<index>], property:<Name>, const:<Full.Type.NAME> (compile-time const field only, never static readonly), or a literal scalar.

attribute.description: Full type name of the assembly-level attribute providing bulk defaults for a project or bounded context.

base_type.description: Full name of a base type or interface. Types deriving from or implementing this are assigned the declared role/metadata.

metadata.description: Inheritance evidence carries no constructor/property arguments; values must be literal scalars or const:<Full.Type.NAME> references to a compile-time const field (never static readonly).

namespace.description: Same namespace glob syntax already accepted by layers.<name>.namespace.

namespace_suffix.description: Same namespace-suffix syntax already accepted by layers.<name>.namespace_suffix.

classificationOverride.description: Exactly one of 'type', 'namespace', or 'namespace_suffix' must be declared - scopes are mutually exclusive, not combinable. 'reason' is required whenever 'namespace' or 'namespace_suffix' is the declared scope, and forbidden from substituting for a missing scope.

type.description: Narrow, type-scoped override. Full type name. 'reason' is optional at this scope. Mutually exclusive with 'namespace'/'namespace_suffix'.

namespace.description: Broad, namespace-scoped override. 'reason' is required at this scope. Mutually exclusive with 'type'/'namespace_suffix'.

namespace_suffix.description: Broad, namespace-suffix-scoped override. 'reason' is required at this scope. Mutually exclusive with 'type'/'namespace'.

classificationExclusion.description: Exactly one of 'type', 'namespace', or 'namespace_suffix' must be declared - scopes are mutually exclusive, not combinable.

target_assemblies.description: Assembly names to load and scan. Optional when a metadata-only or discovery-driven policy uses analysis.solution or analysis.projects instead.

assembly_search_paths.description: Additional directories used when resolving target assemblies.

source_roots.description: Source roots used by method-body Roslyn scanning.

unmatched_ignored_violations.description: Behavior when an ignored_violations entry matches no current violation. 'error' (default) fails validation, 'warn' reports but does not fail, 'off' skips detection.

policy_consistency.description: Behavior when the policy-consistency check finds internal contradictions in the policy itself (duplicate IDs, allow/forbid conflicts, independence conflicts, protected-importer conflicts, layer overlaps, unreachable contracts). 'error' (default) fails validation, 'warn' reports but does not fail, 'off' suppresses findings. The check itself always runs.

coverage.description: Behavior when a declared coverage contract finds uncovered, unknown, stale, or empty-input first-party units. 'error' (default) fails validation, 'warn' reports but does not fail, 'off' suppresses findings even for an explicitly declared coverage contract. Backward compatibility does not depend on this default: a policy with no strict_coverage/audit_coverage contracts is unaffected regardless of this setting, because no coverage contract exists to produce a finding.

condition_sets.description: Named preprocessor symbol sets for conditional compilation analysis. Each key is a condition set name (e.g. 'runtime', 'editor'), each value is an array of preprocessor symbols (e.g. ['UNITY_EDITOR']). Only affects Roslyn source/method-body scanning.

default_condition_set.description: Name of the default condition set used when --condition-set is not provided on the CLI. Must match a key in condition_sets. When empty or unset, no preprocessor symbols are defined.

id.description: Stable contract identifier for CLI selection and cross-referencing.

source_type.description: Source type name or narrow glob pattern to suppress.

forbidden_reference.description: Forbidden reference name or narrow glob pattern to suppress.

reason.description: Human-readable migration reason or issue reference.

dependency_depth.description: Evaluation depth for dependency checking. 'direct' checks only immediate references (1 level). 'transitive' follows the dependency graph via BFS and reports violations at any depth.

id.description: Stable contract identifier for CLI selection and cross-referencing.

dependency_depth.description: Evaluation depth for assembly dependency checking. Only 'direct' (the default) is supported in this release; transitive assembly-reference-path resolution is a planned follow-up and is not yet a valid value here.

Evaluation depth for assembly allow-only checking. Only 'direct' (the default) is supported in this release; transitive assembly-reference-path resolution is a planned follow-up and is not yet a valid value here.
Evaluation depth for package dependency checking. Only 'direct' (the default) is supported; there is no resolved package graph to walk transitively.
Evaluation depth for package allow-only checking. Only 'direct' (the default) is supported; there is no resolved package graph to walk transitively.

forbid_public_constants_unless_declared.description: When true, every exported const field must have its fully-qualified name listed in allowed_public_constants, even if its full signature is present in declared_api.

exhaustive.description: When true, the linter checks for sibling namespaces under each container that are not mapped into any declared layer. Unmapped siblings with types produce violations.

namespace.description: Namespace prefix or glob pattern, same syntax as layers.<name>.namespace.

namespace_suffix.description: Optional namespace suffix constraint, same syntax as layers.<name>.namespace_suffix.

include.description: Glob patterns for project/assembly discovery roots.

exclude.description: Glob patterns excluded from include, before coverage exclusion reasons apply.

reason.description: Required justification for excluding this unit from coverage.
Required justification for excluding this unit from coverage.
Required justification for excluding this unit from coverage.
Required justification for excluding this referenced contract from rule-input coverage.
Required justification for excluding this semantic role fact from coverage.

id.description: Stable contract identifier for CLI selection and cross-referencing.

scope.description: Which kind of first-party unit this coverage contract classifies.

roots.description: Units to classify (the scan boundary the contract classifies, not coverage evidence by itself). Required for, and only valid with, scope: namespace, project, or assembly. Item shape depends on scope: namespace uses coverageNamespaceRoot, project/assembly use coverageDiscoveryRoot (see scope-specific allOf constraints below).

between.description: Declared-layer-name pairs to classify. Required for, and only valid with, scope: dependency_edge.

contract_ids.description: Contract IDs whose rule inputs are checked for stale/empty-input status. Required for, and only valid with, scope: rule_input.

exclude.description: Excluded units, each carrying a required reason. Item shape depends on scope: namespace uses coverageNamespaceExclusion, project uses coverageProjectExclusion, assembly uses coverageAssemblyExclusion, rule_input uses coverageContractIdExclusion. Not valid for scope: dependency_edge (no exclusion shape defined for that scope yet).
